sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
sources.population: Adults, children, and young people with established Crohn’s disease.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
sources.applicability_note: Supports symptom, relapse, treatment-monitoring, perianal, nutrition, prior surgery, patient-view, and follow-up documentation without assigning activity or generating treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.2.11 — information, assessment, and monitoring
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports age-appropriate information, multidisciplinary discussion, symptoms, nutritional status, growth, treatment context, and safety-monitoring records.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.3 — remission and follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording patient views, relapse symptoms, agreed follow-up, access arrangements, and surveillance status.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
sources.population: Adults, children, and young people with established ulcerative colitis.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
sources.applicability_note: Supports symptom, disease-course, treatment, monitoring, growth, bone-health, patient-view, and clinician-entered follow-up documentation without assigning severity or generating management.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1–1.3 — information, treatment context, and review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting current symptoms, previous response, adverse effects, patient preferences, and clinician review context.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.8 — monitoring
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed monitoring results, growth, bone-health context, and communication across services.
